name: Argelès-Gazost and Gaves Greenway Loop
name_original: Petite boucle à partir de Lourdes
description: This difficult 29.8-mile touring cycling loop from Lourdes offers scenic Pyrenean views and significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lourdes offers a diverse landscape at the foothills of the Pyrenees mountains. The region features a blend of challenging mountain passes, scenic valleys, and tranquil lakes. Dedicated cycling routes, such as the Voie Verte des Gaves, run along rivers, providing accessible paths. The terrain includes significant elevation changes, characteristic of the Pyrenean environment, with towering peaks and extensive natural areas. Best touring cycling routes around Lourdes The most popular touring cycling route is Small loop from Lourdes, a 29.8 miles (47.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain with notable elevation gains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Railroad cycle path near Argeles – Argelès-Gazost Station loop from Lourdes, an easy 18.0 miles (29.0 km) path. This trail follows a dedicated cycle path along the Gave de Pau river, offering a relaxed ride through picturesque valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Soulor Pass – Argelès-Gazost Station loop from Lourdes, a 46.9 miles (75.4 km) trail leading through Pyrenean mountain passes, often completed in about 5 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Lourdes is defined by Pyrenees mountains, scenic valleys, and dedicated cycle paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging ascents to more relaxed rides. The routes in Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lourdes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lourdes?
A: There are over 75 touring cycling routes available around Lourdes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging rides through the Pyrenean foothills and valleys.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes around Lourdes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lourdes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 150 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to serene valley rides, and the stunning Pyrenean scenery.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Lourdes?
A: Yes, Lourdes offers several easy touring cycling routes, with 16 routes specifically graded as easy. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24670170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railroad cycle path near Argeles – Argelès-Gazost Station loop from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a dedicated cycle path along the Gave de Pau river, offering a relaxed ride through picturesque valleys.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced touring cyclists expect in the Lourdes region?
A: Advanced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenges, with 43 routes graded as difficult. The region is renowned for its legendary mountain passes, many featured in the Tour de France. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41487817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soulor Pass – Argelès-Gazost Station loop from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt; will test your endurance with significant elevation gains through iconic Pyrenean terrain, including passes like Col du Soulor, Col du Tourmalet, and Hautacam.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that avoid significant elevation gains?
A: Yes, for those preferring flatter terrain, the Voie Verte des Gaves (Greenway of the Gaves) offers a refreshing and re-energizing ride. This dedicated cycle path runs along the Gave de Pau river, connecting Lourdes to Pierrefitte-Nestalas and further towards Gavarnie, providing a gentle gradient ideal for electric bikes and more relaxed touring.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options near Lourdes?
A: Absolutely. The Voie Verte des Gaves is an excellent choice for families, offering a safe and scenic dedicated cycle path. Additionally, the Pic du Jer, accessible by a funicular, features a mountain cycling piste specifically designed for families, providing stunning views without extreme difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Lourdes?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Lourdes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e860410706/small-loop-from-lourdes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small loop from Lourdes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route featuring varied terrain and notable elevation gains.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes around Lourdes offer breathtaking natural beauty and significant landmarks. You can expect panoramic views of the Pyrenees Mountains, serene glacial lakes like Lac de Lourdes, and the picturesque Gave de Pau river. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic du Jer&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pibeste Peak&lt;/a&gt; in the Pibeste-Aoulhet massif, known for its diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, Lourdes is rich in cultural heritage. While cycling, you can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lourdes sanctuary estate&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Lourdes&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through charming villages in the Pays de Lourdes, allowing you to discover the rich heritage of the Pyrenees.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lourdes?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Lourdes generally spans from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically more stable, and mountain passes are clear of snow, offering ideal conditions for exploring the Pyrenean landscape. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there options for public transport access to cycling routes?
A: Lourdes is well-connected, and some routes, particularly those along the Voie Verte des Gaves, are accessible from town. The Pic du Jer is directly accessible via a century-old funicular, which can also transport mountain bikes, making it a convenient starting point for routes on the mountain. For more detailed information on local transport options, you may find resources on the official Lourdes tourism website helpful: &lt;a href="https://en.lourdes-infotourisme.com/explore/enjoy-a-wealth-of-sports-activities/bike-ride-through-the-pyrenees/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lourdes Info Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with other outdoor activities in the region?
A: Absolutely. The Lourdes region is a hub for diverse outdoor adventures. Beyond cycling, you can enjoy hiking in the Pyrenees, paddleboarding or kayaking on Lac de Lourdes, or even more adventurous activities like rafting and canyoning on the Gave de Pau. The area's natural parks, like the Pibeste-Aoulhet massif, are also perfect for wildlife observation. For more ideas, explore the outdoor activities section on the Lourdes tourism site: &lt;a href="https://en.lourdes-infotourisme.com/explore/enjoy-a-wealth-of-outdoor-activities/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lourdes Outdoor Activities&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration to complete the Small loop from Lourdes?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route, covering approximately 48 km (29.8 miles), typically takes around 3 hours and 7 minutes to complete. This duration is for a continuous ride and does not include stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Small loop from Lourdes is categorized as a 'difficult' touring bicycle route. It's considered a 'Hard bike ride' requiring 'very good fitness' due to significant elevation changes typical of the Pyrenean foothills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this loop?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain with notable elevation gains. Most surfaces are paved, offering a diverse landscape that includes challenging mountain passes, serene valleys, and expansive natural areas. You'll experience the classic Pyrenean cycling environment.

faqs.question: What are some scenic highlights or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy views of the Gave de Pau river and the majestic Hautacam and Pibeste mountain ranges. Specific highlights include Beautiful. How beautiful our countryside is., the Bridge over the Gave de Pau, the charming settlement of Saint-Savin in Lavedan, and The Chapel.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Small loop from Lourdes incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it uses significant portions of the Voie Verte des Gaves, and also crosses parts of the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 02 (Lourdes-Tarbes) and the Chemin du Piémont Pyrénéen.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Pyrenean foothills, the best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October) when the weather is generally more stable, and mountain passes are clear of snow. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is parking available near the starting point in Lourdes?
faqs.answer: While the exact starting point and dedicated parking for this specific loop aren't detailed, Lourdes offers various parking options within the town. As this is a loop, you can typically start and finish at any convenient point in Lourdes where parking is available.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily for touring bicycles on paved surfaces. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on leashes in general public areas, it's important to consider their comfort and safety, especially given the route's difficulty and traffic conditions. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and does not interfere with other cyclists or road users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially since it's near Parc National des Pyrénées?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Small loop from Lourdes itself. While the route is in the vicinity of the Parc National des Pyrénées, you are not entering the core zone of the park where specific regulations might apply. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Small loop from Lourdes is not suitable for families with young children or beginners. It is classified as a 'difficult' route requiring 'very good fitness' due to its significant elevation gains and challenging terrain. Beginners or families looking for easier options should explore other, less demanding routes in the Lourdes area, such as sections of the Voie Verte des Gaves.
